summary: Complete implementation of post-write invalidation guard. All 22 steps + 2 prerequisites done. 28 new tests in test_invalidation_guard.py, all passing (71 total with existing tests). Files created: invalidation_guard.py (detect_spurious_invalidations + InvalidationGuard). Files modified: graphiti_client.py (restore_edge_validity, bulk_restore_edge_validity, guard integration in add_episode), config/schema.py (invalidation_guard_enabled field), tests/conftest.py (MockNode.uuid field).

Add invalidation_guard_enabled field to GraphitiBackendConfig in config/schema.py (default True); Add MockNode.uuid field to conftest.py MockNode dataclass (needed for entity-overlap checks in tests); Write failing test: detect_spurious_invalidations() identifies invalidated edges with no entity overlap — an edge with expired_at set whose source/target UUIDs are NOT in the episode's entity set should be flagged as spurious; Implement detect_spurious_invalidations() in invalidation_guard.py — iterate results.edges, collect those with expired_at set and neither source_node_uuid nor target_node_uuid in the set of episode entity node UUIDs; Write failing test: detect_spurious_invalidations() does NOT flag edges that share at least one entity endpoint with the episode (legitimate invalidation); Verify step-3 passes with existing implementation (the entity-overlap check should naturally handle this); Write failing test: detect_spurious_invalidations() ignores edges without expired_at set (non-invalidated edges pass through); Verify step-5 passes (the expired_at check should already filter these out); Write failing test: GraphitiBackend.restore_edge_validity() executes correct Cypher to clear invalid_at and expired_at for a given edge UUID; Add restore_edge_validity(uuid) method to GraphitiBackend — Cypher: MATCH ()-[e:RELATES_TO {uuid: $uuid}]->() SET e.invalid_at = NULL, e.expired_at = NULL; Write failing test: GraphitiBackend.bulk_restore_edge_validity() handles multiple UUIDs and returns count of restored edges; Add bulk_restore_edge_validity(uuids) method — batch Cypher update with pre-count pattern (matching existing bulk_remove_edges style); Write failing test: InvalidationGuard.guard() detects spurious invalidations and calls bulk_restore_edge_validity() to reverse them, returning list of restored UUIDs; Implement InvalidationGuard.guard() — orchestrate detect_spurious_invalidations() + bulk_restore_edge_validity(), log all restorations with edge UUID and fact text; Write failing test: InvalidationGuard.guard() is a no-op when all invalidations are legitimate (all invalidated edges share entity overlap); Verify step-13 passes — guard should return empty list when no spurious invalidations found; Write failing test: InvalidationGuard.guard() is a no-op when results have no edges with expired_at set (clean episode with no invalidations); Verify step-15 passes — early return when no invalidated edges detected; Write failing test: GraphitiBackend.add_episode() calls InvalidationGuard.guard() on the result and logs any restorations; Integrate InvalidationGuard into GraphitiBackend.add_episode() — construct guard from backend, call guard() on AddEpisodeResults, log restorations; Write failing test: InvalidationGuard is skipped when invalidation_guard_enabled=False in config; Add config check in GraphitiBackend.add_episode() — skip guard when config.graphiti.invalidation_guard_enabled is False; Write failing test: InvalidationGuard.guard() handles mixed results — some edges spurious, some legitimate — only restores spurious ones; Verify step-21 passes with existing implementation (detection and restoration are independent per edge)
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues

Complete guard boundary robustness (steps 23-26 + pre-3). Added invalidation_guard_timeout_seconds to GraphitiBackendConfig (default 30.0) and _guard_timeout to GraphitiBackend.__init__. Wrote failing tests for: guard RuntimeError caught non-fatally (step-23), guard invoked via asyncio.wait_for with _guard_timeout (step-25). Implemented: try/except Exception around guard call (step-24), asyncio.wait_for wrapping guard.guard() with _guard_timeout (step-26). All 29 tests in test_invalidation_guard.py pass; 649 total tests passing. All plan steps now done.
